sections.overview: The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports, Government of India confers the Tenzing Norgay National Adventure Award to recognize achievements in adventure activities and to encourage qualities such as endurance, risk-taking, teamwork, and quick reflexes.
sections.objective: To recognize individuals for outstanding achievements in adventure fields and to motivate youth to develop discipline, leadership, and a spirit of adventure.
eligibility: Applicant must have excellent performance in adventure activities
Must demonstrate leadership qualities, discipline, and a sense of adventure
Must have continuous achievements in one field: Land, Air, or Water (Sea)
Awards are given in categories: Land, Water (Sea), Air, and Lifetime Achievement
Generally, only one award is given per category
benefits: Blazer with silken tie or saree
Award status equivalent to Arjuna Awards
Reimbursement of travel expenses (economy airfare or AC 1-tier rail)
Free boarding and lodging during award ceremony
Travel and accommodation support for one companion (for women and differently-abled awardees)
applicationprocess: Submit application in prescribed format with past performance details
Upload recommendation letter from competent authorities if applicable
Serving defence personnel must submit recommendations through their respective directorates
Applications are reviewed and verified by apex institutions based on category
Eligible applications are screened by a committee which recommends candidates
Final decision is made by the Government of India
documents_required: Signed description of achievements as per guidelines
Supporting documents compiled as a single PDF with achievements and recommendation letters
exclusions: Award will not be given twice to the same person in the same category
Government may cancel or annul the award if the recipient is found unworthy
Recipient must return the statuette and award money if the award is cancelled

faq.question: Can a person apply if he/she has already received the award?
faq.answer: Yes, but not in the same category. A person cannot receive the award twice in the same category.

faq.question: Who can apply for the award?
faq.answer: Any Indian citizen working in the field of adventure or a family member of a posthumous adventurer.

faq.question: How is the recommendation letter submitted?
faq.answer: Applicants must collect the recommendation letter and upload a scanned copy along with their application.

faq.question: Can an organization apply for the award?
faq.answer: No, only individuals can apply. Applicants must select the option 'Individual'.

faq.question: What should be selected in the nominator type?
faq.answer: Applicants should select 'Citizen' in the nominator type field.

faq.question: Is recommendation mandatory for all applicants?
faq.answer: It is mandatory for serving defence personnel. Others may provide it if available or mention NA.

faq.question: What documents can be uploaded as supporting documents?
faq.answer: Documents showing year-wise achievements must be uploaded as a single PDF along with recommendation letters, properly indexed and numbered.

faq.question: Does submitting the application confirm eligibility?
faq.answer: No, eligibility is determined after verification and review of the application.
